message:
fix(api): safe f64→isize index conversion for index_select operations

Replaces unsafe f64 comparisons with a new exact_integer_index_to_isize()
helper that performs rigorous float-to-integer conversion:

1. Rejects non-finite values (NaN, Inf)
2. Rejects non-integer values (fractional part check via trunc != value)
3. Range-checks against isize::MIN..isize::MAX before casting
4. Round-trip verifies the cast (converted as f64 == original) to catch
   precision loss for values near i64 boundaries

The index_select dimension size is now converted to isize upfront via
try_from() rather than casting to f64, avoiding precision loss for
dimensions larger than 2^53. Bounds checking uses integer arithmetic
throughout.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
